title: Random errors in Zuul CI when running Podman exec
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Toolbox uses for its CI [Zuul](https://zuul-ci.org/). We've been refactoring our system tests and started to experience random flakes. These happen for the command: `toolbox run sudo id`, which under the hood starts the container, checks if `sudo` exists and then executes the command `sudo id` using `podman exec `. This did not happen in the past, so I'm perplexed, what may be causing this issue.

Here's the pull request where we started to experience this: https://github.com/containers/toolbox/pull/517

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

No local reproducer.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
#   time="2021-02-12T14:09:59Z" level=info msg="podman filtering at log level debug"
#   time="2021-02-12T14:09:59Z" level=debug msg="Called exec.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level debug exec --detach-keys  --interactive --tty --user zuul-worker --workdir /home/zuul-worker --env=DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/run/user/1000/bus --env=LANG=en_US.UTF-8 --env=SHELL=/bin/bash --env=TOOLBOX_PATH=/usr/local/bin/toolbox --env=USER=zuul-worker --env=XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000 --env=XDG_SESSION_ID=1 --env=XDG_SESSION_TYPE=tty fedora-toolbox-33 capsh --caps= -- -c exec \"$@\" /bin/sh sudo id)"
#   time="2021-02-12T14:09:59Z" level=debug msg="Reading configuration file \"/usr/share/containers/containers.conf\""

CgroupCheck:false CgroupManager:systemd ConmonEnvVars:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] ConmonPath:[/usr/libexec/podman/conmon /usr/local/libexec/podman/conmon #   [conmon:d]: exec with attach got start message from parent
```
The effective output of the command is blank.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The output assumes the use of the `registry.fedoraproject.org/f<ver>/fedora-toolbox` image and container created using `toolbox create`.

```
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The issue could not be reproduced locally, so it *most likely is* related to the CI environment. It happens quite frequently but not always.

About a month ago this error also happened on a Fedora 31 host. The logs are no longer available.

**Output of `podman version`:**

The CI is not set to show this information. Can provide later.

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

The CI is not set to show this information. Can provide later.

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
# Fedora 32: podman-2.2.1-1.fc32.x86_64
# Fedora 33: podman-2.2.1-1.fc33.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Zuul CI, Fedora Cloud images

comments.body: would it be possible to check with podman-3 currently in updates-testing for fedora?
Currently the CI is broken due to GPG issues in the Rawhide image we use. When the issues goes away, I'll give you an update.

I ran the pipelines in the CI several times today and it did not fail a single time. I checked the versions of packages and did not notice any change. I'll close this for now and assume it was just the CI environment doing something funky.
